title: Streams, Super Streams, and Workload Choice · Runtime Recall
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs streams, super streams, and workload choice from producer, broker, queue, delivery, Go runtime, and stakeholder evidence, then challenges one protocol or concurrency misconception. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare destructive queue consumption with retained stream offsets, replay, retention, protocol clients, and consumer responsibility; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Define age and byte retention, segment behavior, start offset, consumer progress, replay authorization, and late-reader expectations; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Use super streams to partition throughput by key while accounting for partitions, routing, per-partition order, skew, and consumer coordination; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Workload Selection Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go messaging team handles case 3290 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Workload Selection Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Workload Selection Evidence through the concrete a queue-versus-stream Go client decision record case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Workload Selection Evidence, keep Workload Selection Evidence connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Workload Selection Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Workload Selection Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Workload Selection Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go messaging team handles case 3290 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to select queue, stream, or super stream from retention, replay, throughput, and partition evidence; keep message identity out of metric labels while preserving causal traces; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Record what required effort · Review Workload Selection Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One RabbitMQ data type is always best if tuned with enough memory and prefetch. In Go, amqp091-go queue consumption automatically exposes every stream protocol capability.
